about.index.metadata.title: About
about.index.title: About 6529
about.memeLab.hero.title: Meme Lab
about.memeLab.hero.description: An experimental CC0 contract where Meme artists can test what comes next.
about.memeLab.overview.title: Overview
about.memeLab.overview.intro: The Meme Lab is an experimental CC0 contract for artists who have already minted a Meme Card. They can use the Meme Lab contract to mint NFTs that they like, in any way that they like.
about.memeLab.overview.cc0.title: Experimental by design
about.memeLab.overview.cc0.body: A CC0 contract built to give established Meme artists room to try new ideas.
about.memeLab.overview.artistLed.title: Artist-led
about.memeLab.overview.artistLed.body: Artists decide what to mint and how each experiment should take shape.
about.memeLab.overview.community.title: Open format
about.memeLab.overview.community.body: Art, community, edition size, price, and other dimensions are all open to exploration.
about.memeLab.how.title: How Artists Use the Lab
about.memeLab.how.intro: The contract sets a simple starting point, then leaves the creative decisions to each artist.
about.memeLab.how.eligibility.title: Start with The Memes
about.memeLab.how.eligibility.body: Artists who have already minted a Meme Card can use the Meme Lab contract.
about.memeLab.how.experiment.title: Choose the experiment
about.memeLab.how.experiment.body: Artists are actively encouraged to experiment on any dimension they like: artistic direction, community, edition size, price, and more.
about.memeLab.how.learn.title: Learn in public
about.memeLab.how.learn.body: We will learn more in the next six months about how artists plan to use the contract and what happens.
about.memeLab.outcomes.title: Room for every outcome
about.memeLab.outcomes.body: We hope to see some successes, and certainly expect there will be some failures too.
about.gradient.hero.title: 6529 Gradient Collection
about.gradient.hero.description: One hundred and one pieces that express the 6529 symbol across the full grayscale spectrum.
about.gradient.overview.title: Overview
about.gradient.overview.intro: The Gradient Collection is a collection of 101 NFTs. They were auctioned to collectors in 2021 and 2022.
about.gradient.overview.pieces.title: 101 NFTs
about.gradient.overview.pieces.body: The collection runs from token #0 through token #100.
about.gradient.overview.auction.body: The collection was auctioned to collectors across 2021 and 2022.
about.gradient.overview.onChain.title: On-chain by default
about.gradient.overview.onChain.body: Each of the 100 static pieces is a 100% on-chain SVG with a secondary IPFS link.
about.gradient.preview.alt: Grid of 6529 Gradient artwork progressing from white to black
about.gradient.preview.title: The full grayscale system
about.gradient.preview.description: The 6529 symbol moves through one-percent increments, with the background changing at the midpoint.
about.gradient.design.title: Design
about.gradient.design.system.title: Black ↔ white
about.gradient.design.system.body: The collection represents the 6529 symbol in its two stark black (#100) and white (#0) forms, with 98 grayscale gradients in between.
about.gradient.design.artist.title: The artist’s preferred interpretation
about.gradient.design.artist.body: This is 6529er’s preferred interpretation of the work and the vision for it in its purest form. It recalls Chromie Squiggles Perfect Spectrums: much less flashy than the HyperRainbows, but an iykyk choice.
about.gradient.design.sequence.endpoints: Token #0 presents the symbol in white and token #100 presents it in black.
about.gradient.design.sequence.steps.bod: Each grayscale gradient is a one-percent increment in darkness between 100% black and 100% white, with the background switching from white to black from #49 to #51.
about.gradient.design.sequence.fifty.bod: Token IDs #0 through #100 make 101 tokens, not 100. The special #50 is built differently: it is a GIF and, like the squiggles, it moves.
about.gradient.museum.title: Held by 6529 Museum
about.gradient.museum.permanent.title: Permanent Collection
about.gradient.museum.permanent.body: Gradients #0, #50, and #100 are in the 6529 Museum Permanent Collection.
about.gradient.museum.held.title: Held for now
about.gradient.museum.held.body: Gradients #10, #20, #30, #40, #50, #60, #70, #80, and #90 will be held by 6529 Museum for now.
about.gradient.purpose.title: What Do the Gradients Do?
about.gradient.purpose.answer: They are a graphic expression of the 6529 logo.
about.gradient.purpose.community: The Gradients do not do anything. We encourage social experimentation in the Gradient community, as the collectors are likely to be strong supporters of decentralization.
about.subscriptions.hero.title: Subscription Minting
about.subscriptions.overview.title: Overview
about.subscriptions.overview.intro: Subscription Minting is another way to mint Meme Cards
about.subscriptions.overview.gasSavings: With up to 98% significant gas savings and/or
about.subscriptions.overview.awayFromCom: While being away from your computer at the time of mint and/or
about.subscriptions.overview.setAndForge: On a "set it and forget it" basis for whole SZNs
about.subscriptions.overview.notMintpass: You can decide to mint or not mint any specific Meme Card
Subscriptions respect the allowlist and phase process: A subscription only allows you to mint within the Phase you would otherwise be eligible for
It is better to think about subscriptions as "remote minting"
about.subscriptions.overview.regularMint: Subscriptions are not a replacement for the regular minting process:
You can still mint in the normal manner if you don't subscribe
Subscriptions are an alternative method of minting for those who choose to use them
about.subscriptions.how.title: How it Works
about.subscriptions.reference.funding.se: You can top up your minting balance at any time by sending ETH to: seize.6529.eth from any wallet of your consolidation
about.subscriptions.reference.funding.no: Any amount sent to seize.6529.eth is completely non-refundable:
you can choose which cards to mint but we do not have the capacity to send ETH back
about.subscriptions.reference.funding.ca: The calculator on your profile will calculate the correct amount to send for any number of cards, with options for the remainder of the SZN and remainder of the year
about.subscriptions.reference.funding.pr: The balance will appear on the site profile associated with the sending ETH address
about.subscriptions.reference.funding.de: Top ups must be received by 00:00 UTC (eg, 19:00 EST) the day before the Meme Card mint to be eligible for the mint; otherwise they will roll over to the next mint
about.subscriptions.reference.modes.auto: Automatic Mode: By default, you will auto-mint (get airdrop for) as many Meme Cards as you are eligible for, until your balance is used
about.subscriptions.reference.modes.optO: You can choose to opt-out of any Meme Card mint:
You must turn off minting for that specific drop before 00:00 UTC (eg, 19:00 EST) the day before the mint
about.subscriptions.reference.modes.reve: The card reveal will move forward one day so that you can see the card before making a decision to mint it or not
about.subscriptions.reference.modes.sche: This means that you can opt out on Sunday (for Monday's card), Tuesday (for Wednesday's card) and Thursday (for Friday's card)
about.subscriptions.reference.modes.manu: You can choose to move your whole profile to "manual" as opposed to "automatic": in this case, you will not mint any cards unless you manually opt-in to the specific cards you want to selectively mint
about.subscriptions.reference.delegation: Your card will be airdropped according on the following criteria:
If no delegation, to the primary address of a consolidation
If no consolidation, to the address you send the ETH from
If there is a delegation, in this order:
The delegated address for The Memes for use case "Airdrop"
The delegated address for The Memes for use case "All"
The delegated address for Any Collection for use case "Airdrop"
The delegated address for Any Collection for use case "All"
If you have the suggested TAP configuration with a vault/warm/hot consolidation with your vault delegating ALL to warm or hot, and you would like your airdrops directly to your vault, we recommend:
sending ETH to seize.6529.eth from warm or hot address
adding delegation use-case "Airdrop" for the "The Memes" from that address to your vault

If you have a single address (not consolidated) that has delegated "All" to a minting address and you want to send ETH from the original address and have the airdrop received by that same address, then you should change your delegation to "Minting" otherwise the airdrop will go to your minting address
about.subscriptions.reference.phases.sam: Subscription Minting respects the same Allowlist and Phases as regular minting
about.subscriptions.reference.phases.zer: If you are in Phase 0, subscription minting will automatically airdrop you the card at the beginning of Phase 0: since Phase 0 is underallocated, you are guaranteed a mint
about.subscriptions.reference.phases.one: If you are eligible for Phase 1 or Phase 2, you will be airdropped the card at the beginning of Phase 1 or Phase 2
about.subscriptions.reference.phases.una: In the event that no cards are available to mint in that phase, you will not be airdropped the card and your balance will remain available
about.subscriptions.reference.phases.ord: In the event that at the beginning of a Phase, there are more subscription mints than available cards, the cards will be airdropped in order of when the subscription payment was received
about.subscriptions.reference.phases.gua: As with regular minting methods, you are only guaranteed a mint in Phase 0
about.subscriptions.reference.phases.pop: For popular mints, your opportunity will be determined by your phase, how many other subscription mints are in place for that mint, and when you subscribed relative to other subscribers
about.subscriptions.reference.gas.title: Gas Savings
about.subscriptions.reference.gas.contex: Meme Cards are relatively inexpensive ({cardPrice} ETH) so in periods of high Ethereum gas, minting costs can become a substantial % of the card's cost
about.subscriptions.reference.gas.gwei20: At gwei of {gwei}, they make up {percent} of the card's cost
At gwei of {gwei}, they make up {percent} of the card's cost
about.subscriptions.reference.gas.transf: ETH transfers are the lowest cost transaction on the Ethereum network
about.subscriptions.reference.gas.oneCar: Someone who subscription-mints 1 card at a time will save approximately {percent} in gas costs
about.subscriptions.reference.gas.tenCar: Someone who subscription-mints 10 cards at a time (with one ETH transfer) will save approximately {percent} in gas costs
about.subscriptions.reference.gas.absorb: The Memes will absorb the gas cost of the airdrop internally, so it will not be charged to the collector
about.subscriptions.reference.remote.bus: Many collectors have busy schedules and may not be able to be available three times per week at the time of the mint, due to personal or professional commitments
about.subscriptions.reference.remote.sep: Subscription minting allows them to separate the decision of if they should mint a series of Meme Cards or a specific Meme Card from the specific time of the mint
about.subscriptions.reference.remote.tim: This is a benefit to all collectors, but especially to those who live in time zones that do not overlap well with the minting time
about.subscriptions.reference.reportLead: You can monitor aggregate projected and redeemed subscription counts in the
